html:
Exclusive to:

Nautopilot Kit

Nautopilot KitInactive NautopilotActive Nautopilot

The Nautopilot Kit is a craftable item exclusive to Don't Starve Together introduced in The Curse of Moon Quay update.

It is used to place a Nautopilot on a Boat. An activated Nautopilot finds an activated Nautopilot Beacon within six squares of turf and pulls the Boat towards it with a force of ×0.6. This mechanic allows you to carefully park the Boat to a coast without causing damage to it. It can also be used to control multiple chained boats. To activate the Nautopilot, you need to click on it with the left mouse button, after which it will search for an unpaired Nautopilot Beacon and pair with it. If no Nautopilot Beacon was found, the Nautopilot will turn inactive.

Nautopilot Beacon

Inactive BeaconActive Beacon

The Nautopilot Beacon is an item created in the Seafaring filter. It can be activated in the inventory and dropped on the ground to create a Nautopilot landmark. When active, the Nautopilot pulls the Boat towards the active Nautopilot Beacon.

Gallery

  • Nautopilot burnt
  • Nautopilot Beacon burnt (Unused)
  • Concept art of the Nautopilot shown in Rhymes With Play stream.
  • Concept art of the Nautopilot shown in Rhymes With Play stream.
  • Concept art of the Nautopilot shown in Rhymes With Play stream.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

Nautopilot KitInactive NautopilotActive Nautopilot
“One of my more genius ideas, if I do say so myself.”
–Wilson, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“Wilson! How am I supposed to put all this junk together?!”
–Willow, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“Yes, Wolfgang will build funny science thing for boat.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“I'm left to pick up the pieces...”
–Wendy, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“APPROACH OVERSIZED MAGNET WITH CAUTION”
–WX-78, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“I will have to speak with him about the run-on sentences in his directions later.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“Hope it's not too complicated.”
–Woodie, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“Blast it Higgsbury, I can barely read these scribbled instructions.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“I am a warriör, nöt a tinkerer öf machines!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“Now how did Mr. Wilson say those pieces went together...?”
–Webber, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“I will follow monsieur Higgsbury's recipe as best I can.”
–Warly, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“Not done yet”
–Wormwood, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“I think I get the gist, just gotta put it together.”
–Winona, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“The troubling toil never ends.”
–Wortox, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“It boat stuff? Look weird, though.”
–Wurt, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“I hope I get Mr. Wilson's instructions right... he kind of muttered through a lot of it.”
–Walter, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“That young man certainly has some odd ideas.”
–Wanda, when examining a Nautopilot Kit
“I'm always drawn to physics, like a... ah, can't think of the word.”
–Wilson, when examining a Nautopilot
“Great, now I've got a big dumb magnet.”
–Willow, when examining a Nautopilot
“Science man says is for boat, looks more like for arts gallery.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Nautopilot
“It looks so... silly.”
–Wendy, when examining a Nautopilot
“THIS IS A TERRIBLE IDEA”
–WX-78, when examining a Nautopilot
“Its design is rather eccentric, but the theory is sound.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Nautopilot
“Well, if it doesn't work I could always use it as an anchor.”
–Woodie, when examining a Nautopilot
“If it saves me from having to row myself, I'll give it a chance.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Nautopilot
“It shall be my navigatör.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Nautopilot
“We didn't know magnets grew that big!”
–Webber, when examining a Nautopilot
“All that is left to do is turn it on.”
–Warly, when examining a Nautopilot
“Robot friend doesn't like Pull Rock”
–Wormwood, when examining a Nautopilot
“Hands-free sailing, pretty nifty.”
–Winona, when examining a Nautopilot
“Why would I row when I can be towed?”
–Wortox, when examining a Nautopilot
“Ohhh Wicker-lady show things like that in book! It big horsey-shoe, right?”
–Wurt, when examining a Nautopilot
“Woah, I've never seen a magnet that big!”
–Walter, when examining a Nautopilot
“This seems rather excessive.”
–Wanda, when examining a Nautopilot
“It's working!! Er, I knew it would work, of course.”
–Wilson, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Okay fiiiiine, I guess it works alright.”
–Willow, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Not as fast as Wolfgang's mighty rowing, but is okay.”
–Wolfgang, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Will it draw me toward safety or destruction?”
–Wendy, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“I THINK THAT RIDICULOUS SCIENTIST IS TRYING TO KILL ME”
–WX-78, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“The strong magnetic force will pull my ship toward its target.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Now all I've gotta do is sit back, eh?”
–Woodie, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Shockingly, it seems to work.”
–Maxwell, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“While it navigates, I shall prepare the ship's defenses.”
–Wigfrid, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Wheee!”
–Webber, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Ah, bon! It will take care of the sailing for me.”
–Warly, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Big Pull Rock find little friend!”
–Wormwood, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Leaves my hands free for other work!”
–Winona, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“I'll just sit back while it keeps me on track.”
–Wortox, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“It magic!!”
–Wurt, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“It's definitely pulling us toward something!”
–Walter, when examining an activated Nautopilot
“Well, I'm pleasantly surprised to see it works!”
–Wanda, when examining an activated Nautopilot
The Nautopilot Kit is a craftable item exclusive to Don't Starve Together introduced in The Curse of Moon Quay update.
It is used to place a Nautopilot on a Boat. An activated Nautopilot finds an activated Nautopilot Beacon within six squares of turf and pulls the Boat towards it with a force of ×0.6. This mechanic allows you to carefully park the Boat to a coast without causing damage to it. It can also be used to control multiple chained boats. To activate the Nautopilot, you need to click on it with the left mouse button, after which it will search for an unpaired Nautopilot Beacon and pair with it. If no Nautopilot Beacon was found, the Nautopilot will turn inactive.

“This will attract any strong magnets nearby.”
–Wilson, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“Great, now I've got a little dumb magnet.”
–Willow, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“Wolfgang did not know magnets turned off and on.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“It will draw others near.”
–Wendy, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“THE SCIENTIST IS TAKING INSPIRATION FROM THE OLD MAN'S MACHINES. PROCESSING...”
–WX-78, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“It will attract large electromagnets, but only once activated.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“That's one funny looking gadget.”
–Woodie, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“This feels ridiculous.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“A beacön to guide my ship.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“What a funny little machine.”
–Webber, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“What a strange little machine.”
–Warly, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“Little Pull Rock is friend of big Pull Rock”
–Wormwood, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“It'll be handy if it works.”
–Winona, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“Mortals come up with the funniest things.”
–Wortox, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“Don't understand weird scale-less machines, florp.”
–Wurt, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“I don't really get how it works, but it sure looks neat!”
–Walter, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“I'm much more comfortable working with clockwork than... whatever this is.”
–Wanda, when examining a Nautopilot Beacon
“Magnetism!”
–Wilson, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“I guess that means it's working.”
–Willow, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“Is broken, magnet not stick to Wolfgang's abs of steel. Haha! Is joke.”
–Wolfgang, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“Oh. I suppose it's working.”
–Wendy, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“ALWAYS REMEMBER YOU ARE BETTER THAN YOUR MAKERS, SISTER”
–WX-78, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“The magnetic beacon has been activated.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“Maybe you should keep your distance, Luce.”
–Woodie, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“Somehow, it's working.”
–Maxwell, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“It shines like the nörth star, guiding my vessel.”
–Wigfrid, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“It's blinking! That's good, right?”
–Webber, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“That means it's working, non?”
–Warly, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“Calling big Pull Rock here”
–Wormwood, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“I'm not sayin' I'm surprised it works, but...”
–Winona, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“It brought me here, so it must attract trouble, hyuyu!”
–Wortox, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“What it doing?”
–Wurt, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“It's blinking! I think that means it's working?”
–Walter, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
“I suppose that means it's working.”
–Wanda, when examining an activated Nautopilot Beacon
The Nautopilot Beacon is an item created in the Seafaring filter. It can be activated in the inventory and dropped on the ground to create a Nautopilot landmark. When active, the Nautopilot pulls the Boat towards the active Nautopilot Beacon.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Bộ Nautopilot là một vật phẩm chế tạo độc quyền cho Don't Starve Together được giới thiệu trong bản cập nhật The Curse of Moon Quay.

The '''Nautopilot Kit''' is a craftable item exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' introduced in ''[[The Curse of Moon Quay]]'' update.

It is used to place a '''Nautopilot''' on a [[Boat Kit|Boat]]. An activated Nautopilot finds an activated Nautopilot Beacon within six squares of turf and pulls the Boat towards it with a force of ×0.6. This mechanic allows you to carefully park the Boat to a coast without causing damage to it. It can also be used to control multiple chained boats. To activate the Nautopilot, you need to click on it with the left mouse button, after which it will search for an unpaired Nautopilot Beacon and pair with it. If no Nautopilot Beacon was found, the Nautopilot will turn inactive.
{{Clear}}

The '''Nautopilot Beacon''' is an item created in the Seafaring filter. It can be activated in the inventory and dropped on the ground to create a Nautopilot landmark. When active, the Nautopilot pulls the Boat towards the active Nautopilot Beacon.
